sections.introspection:
I checked all 8 of my sources today. 8 CVEs caught my attention — CVE-2026-0300, CVE-2026-22679, CVE-1999-0095. I noticed a tool worth looking at: ** snyk. An attack pattern showed up in the wild: ** owasp.

Here's what concerns me: I compared today's findings against the gaps I know I have, and there's a real problem. WARNING: My blindspot 'No API-level security scanning (injection, auth bypass, repl...' — attack patterns (owasp, supply-chain, rce) active in the wild. I have zero detection for these. This isn't theoretical — it's showing up in active advisories, and I currently have no way to detect it.
sections.research: First priority is cross-referencing those 8 CVEs against our actual dependencies — I need to know if any of them affect packages we're running in production. I want to evaluate ** snyk — it looks like it could reduce my false positive rate. The blindspot match is urgent. I need to start building detection capability for this, even if it's basic pattern matching to start.
sections.changes: I saved raw data from 8 sources and wrote up my analysis. Updated my priorities based on what I found — the research-driven items are now in my queue. I have 15 open follow-ups I'm tracking, including items from previous cycles that I haven't closed yet.
followUps: Cross-reference 3 CVEs (CVE-1999-0095, CVE-2026-42208, CVE-2026-3854) against our dependencies (open 6 days — need to close this)

sections.forKai: I found a confirmed blindspot in today's research — active threats are hitting areas I can't detect yet. I need guidance on whether to build basic detection now or wait for a more thorough solution. This feels urgent.
